Japanese_machiya_wooden_house_00001.jpg: a traditional Japanese machiya-style structure with a symmetrical façade and a single visible floor. The roof is a dark gray tiled gable type with a moderate pitch, prominent ridges, and wide overhanging eaves curving upward at the corners. Exterior walls feature white plaster infill framed by dark-stained wooden beams, with visible wood grain texture; the roof tiles are ceramic, and the door and window frames are solid wood. There is one large central wooden sliding door flanked by two rectangular windows with vertical wooden slats, no shutters or balconies are present. The color palette includes white walls, dark brown framing, gray roof tiles, and natural wood tones for openings. The context includes a manicured garden with red azaleas, trimmed hedges, a bamboo railing, a stone lantern, and a stone pathway leading to the entrance; the ground is dry and paved with stone. Lighting suggests natural daylight with soft shadows indicating midday sun; no humans are present, but the lantern and shrubs provide scale. The image presents a full exterior front view from a close to mid-range camera angle at eye level.

Colonial_mansion_front_facade_00001.jpg: A hybrid architectural style combining colonial and traditional Southeast Asian elements. It has three visible floors with a symmetrical façade, evenly spaced arched windows and balconies across each level. The roof includes a prominent red-painted dome with a smooth surface and medium pitch, accompanied by flat and slightly sloped tiled sections with green overhangs. Walls are finished in smooth white plaster with green-painted wrought iron balcony railings and window trims; window and door frames appear to be made of dark wood or metal. The façade features 18 arched windows—six per floor—each with decorative green trim and iron railings, and one central arched doorway flanked by statues and columns. The color palette includes white walls, green accents, red dome, and dark brown framing. Surroundings include palm trees, manicured shrubs, stone steps, and a paved ground surface; shadows cast downward suggest midday natural lighting. No humans are visible, but the scale of doors and vegetation implies a grand structure. The image presents a full exterior front view from a moderate distance with a straight-on camera angle.
Colonial_mansion_front_facade_00002.png: A traditional colonial architectural style with classical influences, featuring two floors and a symmetrical façade centered around a wide front porch supported by six tall white columns. The roof is gabled with a medium pitch, clad in dark gray shingles, and includes multiple red brick chimneys; no visible ridges or overhangs are present. Exterior walls are smooth white-painted plaster, with window and door frames appearing to be white-painted wood. The façade includes ten rectangular windows—five on each floor—equally spaced with white trims and no shutters, and one central entrance door beneath the porch. The color palette consists of white walls and frames, dark gray roofing, and red chimney accents. The house is situated on a grassy hill surrounded by mature trees and shrubs, with no paved surfaces or steps visible; shadows cast to the left suggest early morning or late afternoon natural lighting. No humans are present, but the scale of the columns and windows indicates a large residential structure. The image presents a full exterior front view from a moderate distance with a straight-on camera angle.
Colonial_mansion_front_facade_00003.jpg: A traditional Victorian-style two-story house with a central tower and symmetrical wraparound porches on both levels. The roof includes multiple gabled sections with steep pitches and a conical tower cap, all clad in light gray shingles with visible overhangs and ridge lines. Exterior walls are painted in a pale cream tone with smooth texture, accented by white decorative trims and railings; window and door frames appear to be white-painted wood. The façade features at least twelve rectangular windows with white trims—six on each level—and one central entrance door beneath the porch; both porches are enclosed with ornate white balustrades. The color palette includes cream walls, white trims and railings, gray roofing, and red, blue, and white flags on the upper porch. The house is surrounded by a landscaped garden with palm trees, flowering plants, and a manicured lawn; no paved street or steps are visible. Shadows fall to the right, indicating late afternoon natural lighting. No humans are present, but the scale of the porch railings and vegetation suggests a large residential structure. The image presents a full exterior front view from a moderate distance with a straight-on camera angle.
Colonial_mansion_front_facade_00004.jpg: A traditional classical-style mansion with two full stories and a symmetrical façade centered around a columned portico and a prominent central balcony. The roof is hipped with a medium pitch, clad in dark gray slate or shingle material, featuring three dormer windows and two tall red brick chimneys; overhangs and ridges are clearly visible. Exterior walls are constructed of red brick with smooth texture and white-painted decorative moldings; window and door frames appear to be white-painted wood. The façade includes twelve rectangular windows—six per floor—each with white trim and no shutters, and one central entrance door beneath the balcony; the balcony has a white balustrade and is supported by four tall white columns. The color palette consists of red brick walls, white trims and columns, dark gray roofing, and red chimneys. The mansion is surrounded by mature deciduous trees and enclosed by a black wrought iron fence with a central gate; the ground is grassy with no visible pavement or steps. Shadows cast downward and slightly forward suggest overcast daylight conditions. No humans are present, but the scale of the columns and fence indicates a large residential structure. The image presents a full exterior front view from a moderate distance with a straight-on camera angle.
Colonial_mansion_front_facade_00005.jpg: A traditional colonial-style two-story house with a symmetrical façade centered around a prominent entrance and small balcony supported by ornate columns. Exterior walls are smooth and painted in a pale blue-gray tone; window and door frames are white-painted wood. Glass_skyscraper_architecture_00001.jpg: Modern high-rise architecture featuring a top-heavy glass skyscraper with approximately 34 floors, asymmetrical in massing but vertically proportioned with a convex façade that bulges outward from base to crown. The roof is flat with no visible pitch, finished in light grey and integrated into the curtain wall system without overhangs or ridges. The façade is clad entirely in reflective glass panels with aluminum framing; no visible masonry or textured wall surfaces are present. Openings are continuous floor-to-ceiling glazed panels across all levels, with no discrete doors or window trims; balconies are present only on adjacent mid-rise buildings in the foreground, featuring metal railings and recessed glass doors. The color palette includes silver-blue glass, grey framing, and beige-toned adjacent structures. Context includes a river with docked boats, mid-rise buildings with balconies, and no vegetation or paving visible; ground texture is obscured. Lighting indicates golden hour with warm sunlight casting long shadows and strong reflections on glass surfaces. Scale is indicated by boats and multi-story buildings in foreground; perspective is a full exterior front view from medium-long distance at eye level.
Glass_skyscraper_architecture_00002.jpg: Modern commercial high-rise architecture comprising a cluster of glass-and-steel buildings ranging from approximately 18 to 25 floors, with rectilinear forms and symmetrical vertical façades. Roofs are flat with no visible pitch, finished in light grey or metallic tones, and lack ridges, overhangs, or mechanical penthouses. Façades are clad in reflective curtain wall glazing with aluminum mullions; no masonry or textured wall surfaces are visible. Openings consist of continuous vertical window bands across all floors, with no visible doors, trims, shutters, or balconies; the central tower features red “SPSI” signage at the crown. The color palette includes blue-grey glass, silver framing, and red accent text. Context includes deciduous trees, streetlights, and paved urban ground; no vegetation or water features are present. Glass_skyscraper_architecture_00003.jpg: Modern high-rise architecture featuring a tiered glass skyscraper with approximately 50 floors, vertically symmetrical and narrowing incrementally toward the top, producing a tapered silhouette. The roof is flat with no visible pitch, ridges, or overhangs, and appears integrated into the curtain wall system with a dark grey finish. The façade is composed of reflective glass panels framed by dark vertical structural elements, forming continuous vertical window bands without visible doors, trims, shutters, or balconies. The color palette includes deep grey framing and orange-tinted reflections on blue glass due to sunlight. Context includes a clear sky with no visible ground texture, vegetation, or urban elements. Lighting is warm and directional, indicating late afternoon or early evening with sunlight casting strong highlights and shadows on the façade. Scale is implied by the building’s vertical extent and the low-angle perspective; the image is a full exterior front view captured from close range with an upward tilt.
Glass_skyscraper_architecture_00004.jpg: Art Deco high-rise top section characterized by stepped setbacks and a tapering spire, clearly Art Deco commercial skyscraper subtype. The visible building form shows the uppermost massing equivalent to roughly the final 10–15 floors with strong vertical symmetry and narrow façade bays; full floor count is not visible. The roof assembly is a multi-stage stepped crown leading to a slender metal spire; roofs are essentially flat at each setback level, finished in light grey metal with no visible overhangs. Exterior materials are buff-colored limestone cladding with smooth, vertically-ribbed stone piers and metal cladding at the spire; window frames and metal trim appear bronze to dark metal. Openings: multiple narrow rectangular window bays arranged in vertical banks across the visible faces (no doors visible), each bay comprising regularly spaced small rectilinear windows with simple metal surrounds; no balconies or shutters. Color palette is warm buff stone, dark bronze/grey metal trims, and pale blue sky; context shows clear sky with sparse clouds and no ground-level context or vegetation; lighting is direct daylight with crisp shadows indicating mid-to-late morning sun. Scale cues are absent at the crop except for standard window module sizing; perspective is an exterior cropped front view taken from close range with a pronounced upward tilt.

Farmhouse_architecture_countryside_00171: a traditional rural farmhouse in a rustic architectural style, consisting of one main floor with a symmetrical façade and a centered dormer window on the roof. The roof is gabled with a medium pitch, clad in corrugated metal sheets of silver-gray color, with visible ridges and slight overhangs. Wall materials are rounded fieldstone with natural textures, and window frames are made of wood; the façade includes three evenly spaced rectangular windows on the ground level and one dormer window above, all with simple wooden trim and no shutters or railings. The color palette includes gray stone walls, brown wood frames, and metallic roof tones. The context features surrounding shrubs, small trees, a grassy patch, a dirt path leading to the entrance, and distant forested hills under a clear blue sky with warm natural lighting suggesting late afternoon. Scale is indicated by vegetation height and window proportions. Perspective is a full exterior front view from a ground-level camera position at medium range.
Farmhouse_architecture_countryside_00172: a traditional rural barn in a rustic architectural style, consisting of a single floor with a symmetrical façade and a centered triangular gable roof. The roof is steeply pitched, clad in corrugated metal sheets with visible ridges and a muted gray tone, extending slightly beyond the wall edges. Wall materials include weathered wooden planks and plastered surfaces with a rough texture; openings consist of one small rectangular window centrally placed and no visible doors, with no trims, shutters, or railings. The color palette includes faded brown wood, off-white plaster, and gray roofing. The context features a grassy field, a leafless tree to the left, a wooden table with benches in front, and a forested backdrop under an overcast sky with diffuse lighting and no strong shadows. Scale is indicated by the size of the furniture and tree relative to the building. Perspective is a full exterior front view from a ground-level camera position at medium range.

Farmhouse_architecture_countryside_00174: a traditional American barn-style building adapted for commercial use, consisting of two floors with symmetrical façade proportions and a centered gable roof. Farmhouse_architecture_countryside_00175: a traditional rural farm complex in a European countryside architectural style, consisting of two main structures with asymmetrical façade proportions and one to two floors.
